meaning: n.顽童,小孩,[动物]刺猥,[动物]海胆
examples: They say he is a mischief-maker, a tramp, a street urchin—Calumnies!
The spines of a fire urchin form a multicolored flower off Komodo Island, Indonesia.
In the first century A.D., Pliny the Elder wrote of using sea urchin ashes to treat baldness.
Come here for the finest sashimi, sushi, Wagyu beef tartare and slivers of sea urchin.
He recently created an all-orange dish of nasturtium blossoms, sea urchin and sweet potatoes.
